# Security and Data Requirements Checklist: UC-01 Scheduled 311 Data Pull

**Purpose**: Validate whether UC-01 security and data requirements are complete, clear, consistent, and review-ready for PR-level requirements review.
**Created**: 2026-03-12
**Feature**: [spec.md](/root/311-forecast-system/specs/001-pull-311-data/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [X] CHK001 Are authentication requirements specified for every protected trigger and query surface, including run status, current dataset state, and failure notification access? [Completeness, Spec §FR-013]
- [X] CHK002 Are data protection requirements defined for all sensitive operational data classes, including source credentials, run metadata, failure summaries, and current-dataset query results? [Completeness, Gap, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-011, Spec §FR-013]
- [X] CHK003 Are authorization requirements defined for each actor type that needs access, rather than only stating "basic role-based authorization" at a high level? [Completeness, Ambiguity, Spec §FR-013]
- [X] CHK004 Are requirements documented for the full data lifecycle from source retrieval through candidate, stored, and current states, including which data may persist at each stage? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-007, Spec §Key Entities]
- [X] CHK005 Are retention or deletion expectations defined for candidate datasets, failed-run artifacts, and failure notification records, or are those omissions intentional for UC-01? [Completeness, Gap, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-011]

## Requirement Clarity

- [X] CHK006 Is "authenticated access" specific enough to determine what authentication strength or trust boundary is required for backend endpoints? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §FR-013]
- [X] CHK007 Is "basic role-based authorization appropriate for operational users and automated backend processes" defined with enough precision to avoid conflicting role interpretations? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §FR-013]
- [X] CHK008 Is "human-readable failure summary" constrained enough to avoid accidental inclusion of secrets, credentials, or excessive source payload detail in monitoring records? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §FR-011]
- [X] CHK009 Is "validate every retrieved dataset for required structure, required fields, valid data types, and completeness" specific enough to define the minimum acceptance threshold for data quality? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004]
- [X] CHK010 Is "queryable monitoring record store" precise enough to indicate which consumers may query it and what minimum lookup fields must be supported? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §FR-011, Assumptions §UC-01 Scheduled 311 Data Pull]

## Requirement Consistency

- [X] CHK011 Do the endpoint security requirements align with the stated UC-01 scope boundary that excludes dashboard UI and email delivery while still permitting acceptance-visible backend query surfaces? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013, Spec §Assumptions & Dependencies, Plan §Implementation Steps]
- [X] CHK012 Are the candidate-versus-stored-versus-current dataset rules consistent across the functional requirements, key entities, assumptions, and data model? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-007, Spec §Key Entities, Assumptions §UC-01 Scheduled 311 Data Pull]
- [X] CHK013 Do the failure-record requirements remain consistent between the spec, assumptions, and data model for required fields, failure categories, and run linkage? [Consistency, Spec §FR-011, Spec §Key Entities, Assumptions §UC-01 Scheduled 311 Data Pull]
- [X] CHK014 Are cursor-handling rules consistent between first-run behavior, no-new-records behavior, and failed-run behavior so no requirement implies premature cursor advancement? [Consistency, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-008, Spec §Edge Cases, Data Model §Derived Invariants]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [X] CHK015 Can the security-related requirements be objectively reviewed from the written spec alone, or do terms like "authenticated" and "appropriate" still require unstated reviewer assumptions? [Acceptance Criteria, Measurability, Ambiguity, Spec §FR-013]
- [X] CHK016 Can the requirement that no partial or candidate dataset becomes current be objectively verified from the stated invariants and field definitions? [Acceptance Criteria, Measurability, Spec §FR-007, Spec §SC-003, Data Model §Derived Invariants]
- [X] CHK017 Are the minimum required fields for current-dataset queries measurable enough to determine whether the requirement is fully satisfied without inferring extra fields? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §FR-012, Assumptions §UC-01 Scheduled 311 Data Pull]
- [X] CHK018 Do the success criteria trace clearly to the security and data-integrity requirements they are meant to validate, especially for failure visibility and unchanged-current-dataset outcomes? [Traceability, Spec §SC-002, Spec §SC-003, Spec §SC-004]

## Scenario Coverage

- [X] CHK019 Are security requirements defined for both scheduled execution and test-trigger execution of the same ingestion workflow, including any differences in allowed actors? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-013, Research §Decision: Model the scheduler as a production scheduled trigger with a test harness entry point that executes the same ingestion workflow]
- [X] CHK020 Are requirements specified for how sensitive data should be handled across all failure classes, including authentication failure, source unavailability, validation failure, and storage failure? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-011]
- [X] CHK021 Are requirements complete for both first-run full ingestion and incremental ingestion with respect to data integrity, current-dataset continuity, and cursor safety? [Coverage, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-006, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [X] CHK022 Does the spec define whether failure summaries or logs may include malformed source records, and if so, what redaction or minimization rules apply? [Edge Case, Gap, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-011]
- [X] CHK023 Are requirements defined for what happens when authorization succeeds but the actor lacks permission for one query surface while retaining access to another? [Edge Case, Gap, Spec §FR-013]
- [X] CHK024 Are requirements explicit about how the system should treat unexpected schema changes from the source in relation to data rejection, monitoring classification, and retention of transient candidate data? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-007, Spec §FR-011]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [X] CHK025 Are confidentiality requirements defined for source credentials and any operational metadata that could reveal protected backend access patterns? [Non-Functional, Security, Gap, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-013]
- [X] CHK026 Are integrity requirements defined for persisted operational records so reviewers can tell which fields must remain authoritative across runs, notifications, and current-dataset queries? [Non-Functional, Data Integrity, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-011, Spec §FR-012, Data Model §Overview]
- [X] CHK027 Are auditability requirements for security-relevant events specified clearly enough to distinguish access-control failures from ingestion-data failures in logs and monitoring records? [Non-Functional, Observability, Gap, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-011, Spec §FR-013]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [X] CHK028 Are trust assumptions about the canonical Edmonton 311 source explicitly documented, including whether upstream data is treated as untrusted until validation completes? [Assumption, Dependency, Gap, Spec §Assumptions & Dependencies, Research §Decision: Treat the Edmonton Socrata 311 endpoint as the only UC-01 source and normalize its response before validation and storage]
- [X] CHK029 Are assumptions about the monitoring record store, storage system, and scheduler security boundaries documented strongly enough to avoid hidden requirements leakage into implementation? [Assumption, Dependency, Spec §Assumptions & Dependencies, Assumptions §UC-01 Scheduled 311 Data Pull]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [X] CHK030 Is there any unresolved ambiguity about whether "queryable" data surfaces are intended for operational humans only, automated processes only, or both? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-012, Spec §FR-013]
- [X] CHK031 Do any requirements conflict between minimizing persisted invalid data and preserving enough failed-run evidence for monitoring and diagnosis? [Conflict, Spec §FR-007, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-011, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [X] CHK032 Is a traceable requirement scheme in place to connect each security and data-integrity expectation back to a specific requirement, assumption, or success criterion without gaps? [Traceability, Spec §Functional Requirements, Spec §Success Criteria, Gap]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Use this checklist to review requirement quality, not implementation behavior
